name: Keystone: Mitochondria Signaling in Physiology and Disease 2026
desc: Keystone Symposium on mitochondria as signaling organelles that control cellular and organismal physiology, and how mitochondrial dysfunction drives disease.
mtor: TIER 3 — Mitochondrial metabolism and nutrient status are tightly integrated with mTORC1 signaling; mitochondrial cues feed into growth control central to the Atlas.
topics: Mitochondrial signaling; metabolites as signals; redox; mitochondria in immunity, cancer and aging.
organizers: Scientific organizers: Navdeep S. Chandel; Aleksandra Trifunovic.
speakers: Organizers: Navdeep S. Chandel, Aleksandra Trifunovic.

name: 7th European Meeting on AMPK & Nutrient-Sensing Pathways 2026
desc: Triennial European meeting on AMPK and nutrient-sensing signalling.
mtor: TIER 2 — AMPK is the principal upstream antagonist of mTORC1.
topics: AMPK and AMPK-related kinase signalling; nutrient sensing; mTOR; cancer, aging and longevity.
speakers: Grahame Hardie, Reuben Shaw

name: Gordon Research Conference: Systems Aging 2026
desc: Gordon Research Conference on Systems Aging — systems-level approaches to the biology of aging across species.
mtor: TIER 2 — Nutrient-sensing and mTOR / insulin-IGF signaling are central pillars of systems-level aging biology.
topics: Evolutionary perspectives on aging; invertebrate/vertebrate models; biomarkers; frailty; reproductive aging; human longevity.

name: CSHL: Mechanisms of Aging 2026
desc: CSHL meeting on the regulation and function of genes and processes controlling cellular and organismal aging.
mtor: TIER 2 — Nutrient-sensing longevity pathways (TOR/AMPK/insulin-IGF) are central.
topics: Aging mechanisms; nutrient sensing (TOR/AMPK); brain aging; cellular biology; longevity interventions.
organizers: Rozalyn Anderson; William Mair; Ashley Webb
speakers: Keynote: Rafael de Cabo. Speakers: Joseph Baur, Jens Brüning, Dudley Lamming, and others.

name: AACR Annual Meeting 2027
desc: The largest cancer research meeting in the world.
mtor: TIER 2 — Principal venue where human data on mTOR-pathway inhibitors surface first.
topics: PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway in cancer; rapalog pharmacology; resistance mechanisms; tumour metabolism.
speakers: Program not yet published.

name: 13th CFATG Scientific Days on Autophagy (Paris) 2026
desc: Annual meeting of the French Association for Autophagy Research (CFATG).
mtor: Features keynote Roberto Zoncu (mTOR/lysosome), Sharon Tooze, and Ian Ganley. Direct mechanistic overlap with Atlas autophagy-mTOR studies.
topics: Autophagy mechanisms; selective autophagy; lysosomal biology; immunity; cancer; aging
organizers: CFATG (Guillaume Beauclair, Aurore Claude-Taupin, Audrey Esclatine)
speakers: Ian Ganley, Carine Joffre, Louis Lapierre, Katja Simon, Sharon Tooze, Roberto Zoncu

name: Keystone: Autophagy — Mechanisms to Therapy 2026
desc: Keystone Symposium covering the roles and mechanisms of autophagy across health and disease.
mtor: TIER 2 — mTORC1 is the master negative regulator of autophagy.
topics: Autophagy machinery; selective autophagy; mTOR inhibition; lysosomal biology; cancer and neurodegeneration.
organizers: Anne Simonsen; Sharon A. Tooze; Dan Baird
speakers: Keynotes: Erika L. Holzbaur, Harald Stenmark. Many others.

name: Keystone Symposia: Autophagy in Health and Disease — From Mechanisms to Therapeutics 2027
desc: Keystone Symposium covering core autophagy machinery, selective autophagy, non-canonical autophagy pathways, and therapeutic exploitation.
mtor: TIER 2 — mTORC1 is the principal negative regulator of autophagy initiation; sessions on lysosomal biology (Zoncu) and autophagy in aging directly relevant.
topics: Autophagosome biogenesis; selective autophagy; lysosomal signaling; non-canonical autophagy; therapeutic modulation.
organizers: Hong Zhang, Susan Ferro-Novick, Hitoshi Nakatogawa
speakers: Keynote: Noboru Mizushima. Invited: Hitoshi Nakatogawa, Sascha Martens, Roberto Zoncu, Ana Maria Cuervo, and others.

name: American Transplant Congress (ATC) 2027
desc: Joint annual meeting of AST and ASTS; largest human sirolimus/everolimus safety dataset.
mtor: TIER 2 — Largest human exposure dataset for sirolimus and everolimus. Supplies the human safety denominator that geroscience trials cite second-hand.
topics: mTOR inhibitors as immunosuppressants; sirolimus/everolimus protocols; long-term safety; metabolic toxicity.
organizers: American Society of Transplantation (AST) and American Society of Transplant Surgeons (ASTS)
speakers: Program not yet published.

name: ASCO Annual Meeting 2027
desc: The largest clinical oncology meeting worldwide.
mtor: TIER 2 — Where mTOR inhibition meets clinical endpoints in humans; everolimus registration trials were reported here.
topics: Everolimus and temsirolimus clinical trials; bi-steric mTORC1-selective inhibitors; toxicity profiles.
speakers: Program not yet published.

name: 2026 TSC World Conference
desc: TSC Alliance world conference spanning research, clinical care, and family programming.
mtor: TIER 1 — TSC is the genetic disease that established the TSC1/TSC2->Rheb->mTORC1 axis; everolimus is approved on a directly genetic mTOR rationale.
topics: TSC genetics and mTORC1 hyperactivation; everolimus in SEGA, renal AML and epilepsy; clinical management; emerging mTOR therapeutics.

name: International LAM Research Conference & LAMposium 2025
venue: Westin Kansas City at Crown Center
desc: The LAM Foundation research and patient conference; sirolimus standard of care in LAM.
mtor: TIER 1 — LAM is the second approved mTOR-inhibitor indication (sirolimus via MILES trial); only disease whose standard of care IS mTOR inhibition.
topics: LAM cell biology; sirolimus efficacy and long-term outcomes; VEGF-D biomarker; clinical trials in rare lung disease.

name: AACR-NCI-EORTC International Conference on Molecular Targets and Cancer Therapeutics 2027
desc: Drug-development-focused conference where first-in-human data on novel targeted agents are reported.
mtor: TIER 2 — RMC-5552 (first bi-steric mTORC1-selective inhibitor) first-in-human data presented here in 2023.
topics: Third-generation (bi-steric) mTOR inhibitors; 4E-BP1 pharmacodynamics; PI3K-AKT-mTOR drugs; phase 1 oncology data.
speakers: 2027 program not yet published.

name: Cell Symposia: Hallmarks of Aging 2026
desc: Cell Press Symposium exploring the cellular and molecular hallmarks of aging.
mtor: TIER 2 — Deregulated nutrient sensing (mTOR, AMPK, IIS) is a canonical hallmark of aging.
topics: Hallmarks of aging; nutrient sensing; autophagy; senescence; gerotherapeutics; metabolism.
organizers: Rafael de Cabo; Ana Maria Cuervo; Cell Press editors
speakers: Keynote Luigi Ferrucci; Berenice Benayoun, Haim Cohen, and others.

name: AGE 54th Annual Meeting 2026 (American Aging Association)
desc: 54th Annual Meeting of the American Aging Association. Theme: Geroscience: From Discovery to Application.
mtor: TIER 2 — Flagship US geroscience meeting; nutrient-sensing/mTOR biology and rapamycin-class interventions are recurring core topics.
topics: Geroscience; nutrient-sensing and mTOR biology; caloric restriction mimetics; aging models; biomarkers.
speakers: Steven Austad, Rafael de Cabo, Alessandro Bitto, Vishwa Deep Dixit, and others.

name: EMBO Workshop: Autophagy — Foundations, Functions and Physiology 2026
desc: EMBO Workshop focused on advances in autophagy mechanism and selective autophagy.
mtor: mTOR is a primary regulator of autophagy induction; speakers include Roberto Zoncu (lysosome/mTOR).
topics: Selective autophagy mechanisms; ATG proteins; lysosomal biology; metabolism; immunity; cell differentiation
speakers: Ian Ganley, Sharon Tooze, Roberto Zoncu, Katja Simon, Louis Lapierre, Carine Joffre

name: ARDD 2025 — Aging Research and Drug Discovery (12th)
desc: The 12th Aging Research and Drug Discovery meeting — the largest translational geroscience conference.
mtor: TIER 2 — Rapamycin/mTOR inhibition is one of the central pillars of aging pharmacology.
topics: Geroscience; drug discovery; senescence; rapamycin & mTOR; metabolic aging; clinical translation.
speakers: David Sinclair, Marco Demaria, Kristen Fortney, and others.

name: Gordon Research Conference: Autophagy in Stress, Development and Disease 2026
desc: GRC on core autophagy machinery and its roles in cancer, aging, neurodegeneration, infectious disease and development.
mtor: TIER 2 — mTORC1 is the master negative regulator of autophagy; confirmed 2026 program includes "The TFEB-mTORC1 Axis in Tumorigenesis."
topics: Core autophagy machinery; selective autophagy; cancer and immunity; TFEB-mTORC1 axis; lysosome biology; autophagy therapies.

name: Prague Metabolism and Signaling Symposium 2026
venue: National Library of Technology (NTK), Campus Dejvice
desc: Meeting at the intersection of metabolism and signal transduction, co-chaired by David Sabatini and Katerina Rohlenova, with a dedicated mTOR Biology session.
mtor: TIER 1 — Co-chaired by David Sabatini, keynote by Michael N. Hall (discovered TOR), explicit mTOR Biology session.
topics: Energy & metabolite sensing; organellar signaling; autophagy; aging; cancer metabolism; mTOR biology.
speakers: Keynotes: Karen Vousden, Michael N. Hall. Invited: Brendan Manning, Reuben Shaw, Kuang Shen, Mario Pende, David Sabatini, and ~30 others.

name: Keystone Symposia: Healthy Aging & Therapeutic Opportunities 2027
desc: Keystone conference on strategies for promoting healthy aging, biomarkers, and therapeutic development.
mtor: TIER 2 — Co-organized by Brian K. Kennedy, a leading rapamycin/mTOR longevity researcher.
topics: Geromedicine; biomarkers of aging; combination therapies; drug target identification; translational pathways
organizers: Derek Huffman; Kristen Fortney; Brian K. Kennedy; Shu-Chun Teng
speakers: Confirmed speaker roster not yet published.

name: Fifth Geroscience Summit: Revisiting the Geroscience Hypothesis - Focus on Health
desc: NIA-hosted summit bringing together researchers, clinicians, and stakeholders to build a 3-5 year geroscience research roadmap.
mtor: TIER 2 — NIH/NIA flagship geroscience summit; gerotherapeutics and healthspan sessions directly track the Atlas mTOR-aging axis.
topics: Geroscience hypothesis; healthspan; gerotherapeutics; biomarkers of aging; hallmarks; clinical translation
speakers: Keynote: Luigi Ferrucci. Speakers include Joan Mannick, Jamie Justice, John Newman, and many others.

name: Keystone: Tumor Metabolism — New Frontiers in Diet, Microbiome and Beyond 2026
desc: Keystone Symposium exploring the reciprocal relationship between cancer and whole-body metabolism.
mtor: TIER 2 — PI3K-AKT-mTOR axis is a central controller of tumor cell growth and metabolism.
topics: Tumor metabolism; diet and cancer; microbiome; nutrient sensing; therapeutic vulnerabilities.
organizers: Karen H. Vousden; Cynthia L. Sears; Eileen White
speakers: Keynote: Heather Christofk. Invited: Craig B. Thompson, Matthew G. Vander Heiden, Ralph DeBerardinis, and others.

name: 2026 Biomarkers of Aging Conference
venue: Joseph B. Martin Conference Center, Harvard Medical School
desc: Annual conference from the Biomarkers of Aging Consortium.
mtor: TIER 3 — Broad geroscience/biomarkers meeting; several speakers work on nutrient-sensing and metabolic longevity pathways adjacent to the Atlas.
topics: Biomarkers of aging; longevity interventions; epigenetic clocks; multi-omics aging biomarkers; AI tools.
organizers: Vadim Gladyshev, Mahdi Moqri, Chiara Herzog, Andrea Cipriano, Jesse Poganik
speakers: Vadim Gladyshev, Vera Gorbunova, Steve Horvath, Thomas Rando, Eric Verdin, and others.

name: ARDD 2026 — Aging Research and Drug Discovery (13th)
desc: The 13th Aging Research & Drug Discovery (ARDD) meeting, relocated to Harvard University.
mtor: TIER 2 — Rapamycin/mTOR-based interventions are a core geroscience topic.
topics: Geroscience; drug discovery; senescence; rapamycin & mTOR; clinical translation.
organizers: Insilico Medicine; Vadim Gladyshev; Jesse Poganik; Alex Zhavoronkov
speakers: More speakers to be announced.

name: Keystone: Autophagosomes and Endolysosomes 2025
desc: Keystone Symposium spanning autophagosome and endolysosomal biogenesis and function through to disease.
mtor: TIER 2 — The lysosomal surface is the physical platform for mTORC1 activation (Rag GTPases, Ragulator) and the TFEB axis.
topics: Autophagosome biogenesis; endolysosomal trafficking; lysosomal signaling; TFEB; disease implications.
organizers: Christina Towers; Rupert Beale; Leon O. Murphy
speakers: Christina Towers, Rupert Beale, Leon O. Murphy.
